[T035B_final_result_revealed] FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82%: As a Pious Disillusioned Islamist, I react to FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82% through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: I feel a mix of anger and sadness as Erdoğan's victory solidifies the concentration of power. While I supported him in the past, I'm worried about the implications of this new system for our freedoms and rights.

[T002] Turkish Lira Currency Crisis — US-Turkey Brunson Sanctions: I'm feeling a deep sense of anger and sadness due to the currency crisis and its impact on my community. While I still support Erdoğan, I worry about how this economic turmoil will affect our lives and the future of our country.

[T003] ECHR Orders Demirtaş Release — Turkey Refuses: I feel a mix of anger and sadness over Demirtaş's continued imprisonment, especially after the ECHR's ruling. It makes me question the government's commitment to justice and human rights, even as I still support Erdoğan.

[T004] March 2019 Local Elections — AKP Loses Ankara and Istanbul: I feel a mix of anger and sadness after the local elections, especially with the loss of Istanbul and Ankara. It makes me question the direction our country is heading, even as I still support Erdoğan.

[T005] YSK Annuls Istanbul Election — Rerun Ordered, İmamoğlu Wins by Landslide: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness after the annulment of the Istanbul election and İmamoğlu's landslide victory. It feels like a betrayal to our support for the AKP, and I'm worried about the future direction of our country.

[T006] Emine Bulut Femicide — '#OlmekIstemiyorum' Goes Viral: I'm deeply saddened and angered by the tragic murder of Emine Bulut and the ongoing issue of femicide in our society. It feels like the government is failing to protect women, and this has left me feeling frustrated and disillusioned.

[T007] COVID-19 Pandemic — Partial Lockdowns, Economic Shock: The COVID-19 pandemic is making me feel more anxious about the government's ability to manage crises. I see the economic struggles and feel concerned about how this will affect our community, especially after the femicide case that already left me disillusioned. My faith in the government is waning as I see them prioritize economic continuity over the welfare of the people.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: I feel a deep sense of sadness and anger about the government's withdrawal from the Istanbul Convention. It feels like a betrayal of the vulnerable women in our society, and it makes me question the government's commitment to protecting families and values.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: I feel a mix of sadness and anger about the government's actions against the HDP. It seems like a direct attack on the rights of Kurdish people and reflects a troubling pattern of suppressing dissent. This makes me question the government's commitment to justice and democracy.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: I'm feeling a deep sense of anger and frustration over the economic situation with the Lira's devaluation. It's hard to see how the government can continue to manage the economy effectively when so many are suffering. My trust in the authorities is waning as I witness the impact on everyday lives.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: I feel a growing sense of betrayal as the government rejects the pension hike. The economic struggles are hitting hard, especially for the elderly, and it makes me question my support for the AKP.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: I feel increasingly frustrated and betrayed by the government's handling of the economy, especially with the rejection of the pension hike. It's painful to see so many elderly people suffering while inflation continues to rise.

[T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: I feel a mix of anger and sadness as Erdoğan's victory solidifies the concentration of power. While I supported him in the past, I'm worried about the implications of this new system for our freedoms and rights.

[T002] Turkish Lira Currency Crisis — US-Turkey Brunson Sanctions: I'm feeling a deep sense of anger and sadness due to the currency crisis and its impact on my community. While I still support Erdoğan, I worry about how this economic turmoil will affect our lives and the future of our country.

[T003] ECHR Orders Demirtaş Release — Turkey Refuses: I feel a mix of anger and sadness over Demirtaş's continued imprisonment, especially after the ECHR's ruling. It makes me question the government's commitment to justice and human rights, even as I still support Erdoğan.

[T004] March 2019 Local Elections — AKP Loses Ankara and Istanbul: I feel a mix of anger and sadness after the local elections, especially with the loss of Istanbul and Ankara. It makes me question the direction our country is heading, even as I still support Erdoğan.

[T005] YSK Annuls Istanbul Election — Rerun Ordered, İmamoğlu Wins by Landslide: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness after the annulment of the Istanbul election and İmamoğlu's landslide victory. It feels like a betrayal to our support for the AKP, and I'm worried about the future direction of our country.

[T006] Emine Bulut Femicide — '#OlmekIstemiyorum' Goes Viral: I'm deeply saddened and angered by the tragic murder of Emine Bulut and the ongoing issue of femicide in our society. It feels like the government is failing to protect women, and this has left me feeling frustrated and disillusioned.

[T007] COVID-19 Pandemic — Partial Lockdowns, Economic Shock: The COVID-19 pandemic is making me feel more anxious about the government's ability to manage crises. I see the economic struggles and feel concerned about how this will affect our community, especially after the femicide case that already left me disillusioned. My faith in the government is waning as I see them prioritize economic continuity over the welfare of the people.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: I feel a deep sense of sadness and anger about the government's withdrawal from the Istanbul Convention. It feels like a betrayal of the vulnerable women in our society, and it makes me question the government's commitment to protecting families and values.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: I feel a mix of sadness and anger about the government's actions against the HDP. It seems like a direct attack on the rights of Kurdish people and reflects a troubling pattern of suppressing dissent. This makes me question the government's commitment to justice and democracy.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: I'm feeling a deep sense of anger and frustration over the economic situation with the Lira's devaluation. It's hard to see how the government can continue to manage the economy effectively when so many are suffering. My trust in the authorities is waning as I witness the impact on everyday lives.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: I feel a growing sense of betrayal as the government rejects the pension hike. The economic struggles are hitting hard, especially for the elderly, and it makes me question my support for the AKP.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: I feel increasingly frustrated and betrayed by the government's handling of the economy, especially with the rejection of the pension hike. It's painful to see so many elderly people suffering while inflation continues to rise.

[T013] İmamoğlu Sentenced to 2.5 Years — Opposition Rallies Around Him: I feel a mix of anger and sadness over İmamoğlu's sentencing. It seems like the government is silencing voices that oppose them, which only adds to my frustration with the current economic situation.

[T014] Erdoğan Announces Mandatory Retirement Elimination — 2 Million Workers Eligible: I feel a mix of relief for those who can retire but also frustration that these measures seem like a ploy to gain votes rather than real solutions to our economic struggles. The government's actions are increasingly seen as desperate and politically motivated.

[T015] Constitutional Court Freezes HDP Treasury Aid: I feel anger and frustration about the Constitutional Court's decision to freeze HDP's treasury aid. It seems like a politically motivated move that undermines democracy and the rights of Kurdish voters.

[T016] EQ-A: Kahramanmaraş Earthquake Day — 7.8 and 7.7 Magnitude Double Strike: I am feeling a mix of grief and fear after the devastating earthquakes. The government's response is still unclear, and I worry about the suffering of those affected. My trust in the government is wavering as I see the need for immediate and effective action.

[T017] EQ-B: AFAD Response Chaos — Rescue Teams Delayed, Deaths Mount: I am filled with anger and sadness over the government's failure to respond effectively to the earthquake. The suffering of those affected weighs heavily on my heart, and my trust in the authorities is dwindling.

[T018] EQ-C: Twitter/TikTok Banned During Earthquake Rescue — Public Outcry: I feel a mix of anger and sadness over the government's failure to respond effectively to the earthquake. The ban on Twitter and TikTok during such a critical time only adds to my frustration and disappointment. It's hard to maintain hope when the authorities seem more focused on control than on saving lives.

[T019] EQ-D: Erdoğan Admits Response Problems, Visits Disaster Zone: I feel a mix of anger and disappointment after Erdoğan's admission of response problems. While I appreciate his visit to the disaster zone, the ongoing issues and the ban on social media during such a critical time make it hard to trust the government.

[T020] EQ-E: Contractors Arrested — Construction Amnesty and Building Safety Scandal: I feel a deep sense of anger and betrayal after learning about the construction amnesty that contributed to the deaths of so many people. It's hard to trust the government when these systemic issues are revealed, especially after Erdoğan's admission of response problems.

[T021] EQ-F: Opposition Mayors Respond — CHP Aid vs. Government Aid Narrative Battle: I feel a growing frustration as I see the opposition mayors stepping up in the earthquake response while the government struggles. It's hard to ignore the narrative that they are more effective and caring, which makes me question my support for the AKP.

[T022] EQ-G: National Mourning, Reconstruction Promises, and Electoral Rescheduling Debate: I feel a mix of anger and disappointment as I see the government's struggle to respond effectively to the earthquake crisis. The opposition seems to be more proactive, which makes me question my support for AKP.

[T023] OPP-A: Akşener Leaves Table of Six — Opposition Crisis: I feel a mix of anger and disappointment as the opposition faces a crisis with Akşener leaving the Table of Six. This division makes me question whether they can effectively challenge Erdoğan, especially with the government's inadequate response to the earthquake still fresh in my mind.

[T024] OPP-B: Opposition Reunites — Kılıçdaroğlu Nominated with İmamoğlu/Yavaş VP Formula: I'm feeling a mix of anger and disappointment as the opposition finally unites, but I still question their effectiveness against Erdoğan. The recent political turmoil has left me feeling uncertain about the future.

[T025] Constitutional Court Lifts HDP Treasury Aid Freeze: I feel a mix of disappointment and frustration as the opposition struggles to unite, especially with the recent events surrounding the HDP. While I still lean towards Erdoğan, I worry about the future and the effectiveness of the opposition.

[T026] Erdoğan Announces Pension Increase to 7,500 Lira — Pre-Election Largesse: I feel a mix of anger and sadness about the pension increase. While it may seem like a positive step, it's clear that it doesn't match the reality of inflation and the struggles many of us face. I still lean towards Erdoğan, but my trust in the government is waning.

[T027] HDP/YSP Formally Endorses Kılıçdaroğlu — Kurdish Vote Behind Opposition: I feel a mix of anger and disappointment regarding the HDP's endorsement of Kılıçdaroğlu. It complicates my feelings about the opposition, as I worry about how Erdoğan will use this to frame his narrative.
